summary: Strong opening solo, solid set with Mississippi and Bird Song
blurb: The show opens with a scorching Jerry Garcia solo that sets the tone for an energetic night. Highlights include a lively Mississippi Half‑Step Uptown Toodeloo and a beautifully rendered Bird Song. The second set flows through classic jams into a sweet, slowed‑down Mighty Quinn finale.
review: May 11, 1991 at Shoreline Amphitheatre captured one of the band’s tighter late‑summer outings. Jerry Garcia kicks things off with an electrifying solo that many fans rank among his best, lighting up the sky and propelling the group into a punchy Mississippi Half‑Step Uptown Toodeloo. The first set rides a smooth groove through folk‑rock staples before slipping into a heartfelt Bird Song that stands out in the audience recording. In the second set the band strings together Playing In The Band → Uncle John’s Band → Drums → Space → I Need A Miracle → Morning Dew → Around & Around, delivering the kind of seamless segues that keep the crowd smiling. The encore offers a playful, slowed‑down rendition of The Mighty Quinn, showcasing Jerry’s sense of humor and the band’s relaxed vibe to close the night. While some soundboard sources note occasional bassy muddiness and glitches, the overall performance is consistently praised for its energy, tight playing, and memorable moments.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Two recordings describe the performance positively (electrifying opening solo, standout Mississippi and Bird Song), while one is mixed due to technical glitches but still praises the show. No consistent negative comments about playing were found, so a moderate positive adjustment of +1.0 is applied.

best_recording.reason: Crystal‑clear soundboard capture with no audio issues, showcasing the electrifying opening solo and overall top‑class performance.

key_highlights: Electrifying Jerry Garcia opening solo that ranks among his best
Mississippi Half‑Step Uptown Toodeloo opener sets a high‑energy tone
Beautifully rendered Bird Song stands out in the first set
Smooth second‑set flow ending with a playful slowed‑down Mighty Quinn

band_performance.Jerry: Delivers an outstanding opening solo and a humorous, slowed‑down Mighty Quinn that highlight his playful brilliance.
band_performance.Brent: Bruce Hornsby’s piano work is praised as big and rich, adding depth to the set.
